name: Linopy (Linear Optimization with N-Dimensional Data)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); PyPSA project / Fabian Hofmann

auth.notes: Local library; requires solver (GLPK, HiGHS, Gurobi, CPLEX)

performance.notes: xarray-backed variable arrays; vectorized constraint creation; HiGHS free solver recommended

agent_readiness.notes: Pandas/xarray-native linear programming. import linopy. m = linopy.Model(). x = m.add_variables(lower=0, coords=[range(10)], name='x'). m.add_constraints(x.sum() >= 5, name='total'). m.add_objective(x.sum()). m.solve('highs'). Array-valued variables with xarray coordinates — write constraints vectorized over dimensions like numpy. Used in PyPSA (energy system modeling). Solvers: HiGHS (free, excellent), GLPK, Gurobi, CPLEX. Much cleaner for multi-dimensional LP than Pyomo/PuLP.

gotchas: Smaller community than Pyomo or OR-Tools — less Stack Overflow coverage
Primarily developed for energy system optimization (PyPSA use case)
xarray coordinates required for multi-dimensional variables (learning curve)
best_when: Large-scale LP/MIP with multi-dimensional variable arrays (energy, logistics)
Teams already using xarray/pandas for data and wanting LP integration
When PuLP/OR-Tools lack vectorized constraint creation
avoid_when: Simple LP with few variables (PuLP or OR-Tools simpler)
summary: Linopy is a pandas/xarray-native LP modeling library. m.add_variables(lower=0, coords=[range(10)]) creates array-valued variables. m.add_constraints() uses xarray broadcasting for vectorized constraints. m.solve('highs') — HiGHS solver recommended (free, fast). Used in PyPSA energy system modeling. Much cleaner than Pyomo for multi-dimensional LP where variable indices align with xarray coordinates. MIT license.
